headline: Orang Asli Lose 4,200 Hectares to Felda Resettlement Scheme
context: A 2025 COAC report documented 4,200 hectares of Orang Asli customary land in Perak and Pahang absorbed into Felda schemes without consent between 1980 and 2015.

cards.big: Felda is celebrated as Malaysia's most successful land development programme.
cards.sub: 4,200 hectares of that success was built on Orang Asli customary land taken without consent.

cards.big: The Aboriginal Peoples Act 1954 allows the state to relocate Orang Asli 'for any purpose.'
cards.sub: Section 7 vests all Orang Asli reserve land in the state — communities are statutory tenants, not owners.

cards.big: 78 Orang Asli families in Pos Bersih received RM2,000 each for ancestral land.
cards.sub: The same land was valued at RM180,000 per hectare when transferred to Felda settlers.

cards.big: SUHAKAM's 2013 inquiry recommended 'immediate land title recognition.'
cards.sub: 12 years later, fewer than 4% of Orang Asli communities hold registered land titles.

cards.big: RM2,000 per family for land worth RM180,000 per hectare is not compensation. It is confiscation with a receipt.

The Aboriginal Peoples Act 1954 is colonial legislation preserved by an independent state. Its fundamental premise — that indigenous people are wards, not owners — is incompatible with modern rights standards.
